title: Podman on Alpine Linux v0.4.x: reboot causes bugs with CNI.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

After update to 0.4.x (I think it happened with 0.4.1)  on reboot containers with their own network stack (so not using --network=host) throws warnings after running `podman start --all`.

Only the second/third time, makes them start without warnings.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

You need Alpine Latest Stable with podman and cni-plugins installed via apk package manager. (Use some VM to reproduce). Also add podman to default stage `rc-update add podman`. Then on your clean install

1. Set up your `/etc/containers/containers.conf` to use CNI instead of netavark.

2. Create at least one network: `podman network create`

3. Run some detached containers in those networks, then reboot machine.

4. Try to run `podman start --all` with stopped containers.

**Describe the results you received:**
after reboot I have to run `podman start --all` 2-3 times to force my containers to start. Warnings are thrown.

First error not always appears.

Second error is always:
```
WARN[0000] Failed to load cached network config: network cni-podmanX not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network cni-podmanX from disk
WARN[0000] 1 error occurred:
        * plugin type="bridge" failed (delete): cni plugin bridge failed: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s xx.xx.xx.xxx -j CNI-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx -m comment --comment name: "cni-podmanX" id: "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.8 (legacy): Couldn't load target `CNI-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx':No such file or directory

Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information.
```
for each network

_(where X and x are IDs)_

**Describe the results you expected:**
after reboot I have no warnings thrown and my stopped containers starts normally after `podman start --all`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
1. I switched to netavark to check if it behaves in similar way. Surprisingly with netavark network stack, containers starts properly after `podman start --all` 

2. If You run `podman stop --all` before reboot, `podman start --all` works normally after reboot

**Workaround**
_Rebuild your containers on reboot using self-written script._

1. Put this script to /etc/local.d and change its name by adding `.start` extension
2. chmod +x this script
3. run `rc-update add local`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18.2
Git Commit:   73c021e0588393f18d2c54dec335ec9e960287a4

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
It happens on both Virtual Environment and bare metal.

comments.body:
This is not the actual error. These are just warnings because cleanup is not working after setup failed.
Please provide the actual error which is shown after these warnings.

\+1 for the "_Alpine Linux v0.4.x_" in your title @BigB84 :+1:

In the process of looking for [_the oldest version of Alpine I could find_](https://bit.ly/wtFisv04x), TIL the name is actually an acronym :mortar_board:
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I did not get any feedback I am closing this one.
